creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
As I said, opening sqlite DB via network file systems (NFS, samba, sshfs) is not supported by sqlite [1] and while it can work most of the time, it is warranted to corrupt your database sooner or later. It is not required to access it simultaneously from more than one machine to cause corruption of some kind to happen.

Apart from this are you sure windows and FreeBSD are accessing the DB using the same version of the sqlite library? What version of sqlite is embedded in the windows installed calibre?

I don't know the internals of calibre, so for further analysis on this problem I suggest you file a bug report with the upstream.

I am unable to investigate any further without taking a look at the sqlite DB that is stated to be corrupted.


[1] upstream clearly explains this in various places:

https://sqlite.org/whentouse.html

and more technical:

https://sqlite.org/atomiccommit.html

(search for "network")
